evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` imports UI toast (`@/shared/components/ui/toast`) and triggers toast side effects inside a widely shared error helper.

`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` and `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` both depend on that shared helper, so non-UI domain hooks inherit UI coupling through the error boundary.

`src/types/shots.ts` is a deprecated re-export shim, but active code (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts`) still imports through the legacy path, preserving migration-era indirection in a cross-module type boundary.

`src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx` centralizes many feature/theme-specific variants (retro/theme/success) in a global primitive, mixing system-level UI ownership with page/tool-specific visual policy.

`src/pages/Home/components/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx` is a 441-line component containing many embedded mockup subcomponents plus scenario routing logic (`getMainVisual`), concentrating multiple responsibilities in one module.

Touch interaction behavior is split across unrelated layers (`src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMobileInteractions.ts`, and `src/pages/Home/components/CreativePartnerPane.tsx`), making ownership of mobile interaction rules hard to reason about.
`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/applySettingsService.ts` is a compatibility facade that mostly re-exports lower-level services without behavior.
`src/tools/image-generation/components/ImageGenerationForm/index.tsx` is a compatibility re-export shell for shared implementation.
The migration/compat surface is split across wrappers and legacy adapters (`src/types/shots.ts`, `src/shared/components/segmentSettingsMigration.ts`), increasing indirection cost.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts` performs large legacy-to-unified translation branches for structure guidance and then deletes legacy keys, showing boundary normalization is spread into feature code.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` repeats similar compatibility reconstruction/parsing (legacy structure fields, fallback parsing helpers), creating another seam for the same contract translation problem.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` queries sibling/final tasks using multi-path `.or(...)` conditions for `orchestrator_run_id`/`run_id` and `orchestrator_task_id`/`orchestrator_task_id_ref`/nested details, indicating backend seams still depend on mixed contract shapes.

Aspect-ratio style logic is duplicated across `src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts` (`getAspectRatioStyle`) and `src/shared/lib/imageAspectRatio.ts` (`getImageAspectRatioStyle`) with overlapping fallback behavior.
`src/shared/hooks/useUpdatingTimestamp.ts` mixes generic hook behavior with task-specific wrapper behavior in one file, reducing local cohesion.
`src/shared/lib/preloading/preloader.ts` uses repeated best-effort branches/early returns and silent catch paths that keep flow safe but slightly fragmented.

`buildTravelRequestBody` accepts both nested groups (`motionParams`, `generationParams`, `seedParams`) and duplicated flat legacy fields in the same signature, then merges with fallback precedence, creating two competing API shapes in one function (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:463`).

`useGenerationInvalidation` mixes async contracts for similar operations: callback API returns `void` while optionally scheduling delayed work (`useInvalidateGenerations`), `invalidateGenerationsSync` can also defer work via `setTimeout` but still returns `void`, while sibling `invalidateVariantChange` is `async` and awaitable (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:58`, `src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:84`, `src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:105`).

Parallel utility seams expose explicit awaitable lifecycle in one place (`runSerializedTimelineWrite` returns a promise and tracks queue phases), but invalidation utilities for adjacent data flows hide delayed completion, making call-site expectations inconsistent (`src/shared/lib/timelineWriteQueue.ts:108`, `src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:84`).
`src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` uses a long explanatory block (`@example`, multi-context usage notes) around a small hook with straightforward behavior.
`src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts` includes extensive narrative comments that restate field meanings and control flow already visible in code.
`src/shared/lib/storagePaths.ts` and `src/shared/lib/taskConfig.ts` contain heavy banner/comment scaffolding around simple path builders and config lookups, increasing maintenance noise.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` is an explicit compatibility shim with many deprecated fields and a sunset date (`2026-05-31`), indicating old/new payload coexistence is still active.
`src/types/shots.ts` is fully deprecated but still exists as a re-export shim, prolonging legacy import paths.

`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts` retains multiple deprecated legacy fields in `ProjectImageSettings` for migration compatibility, showing migration is intentionally incomplete in active types.

holistic_context.structure.root_files marks `vitest.edge.config.ts` (fan_in 1), `vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts` (fan_in 1), `tailwind.config.ts` (fan_in 0), `vite.config.ts` (fan_in 0), and `vitest.config.ts` (fan_in 0) as low-fan-in peripheral root stragglers with the same build/test concern.

`src/shared/hooks/` has 98 production files in one flat directory (directory_profiles), well above the >10 mixed-concern split threshold; assigned hooks (`useAIInteractionService.ts`, `useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts`, `useApiKeys.ts`) represent different domains (AI orchestration, pagination preloading, credential persistence) but share the same top-level folder.

Test placement is inconsistent inside shared packages: hooks use `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/...` while components use colocated tests like `src/shared/components/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.test.tsx` and `src/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx`, increasing navigation variance.

`useTimelineOrchestrator` combines drag/zoom/selection/drop/endpoint/video-browser orchestration in one 664-line hook with large input/output contracts (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts:21`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts:54`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts:141`).

`useSharedLightboxState` acts as a mega-facade over many concerns (variants, navigation, shot management, lineage, references, layout, button groups) through one composed props type and one consolidated return object (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts:128`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts:602`).

`useImageEditOrchestrator` coordinates four edit modes plus persistence synchronization and context assembly in a single hook, with a broad prop interface and a large memoized context payload (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts:32`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts:128`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts:392`).

Task flow mixes view orchestration and data/cache mutation (`src/shared/components/TasksPane/TasksPane.tsx:39`, `src/shared/components/TasksPane/TasksPane.tsx:239`) while `useTasks` also owns query construction, status-specific sorting, pagination shaping, and data freshness side effects (`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:93`, `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:117`, `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:209`).

Form submission logic passes a wide, repeated prop bag across multiple hooks (`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/types.ts:33`, `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/useFormSubmission.ts:20`, `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/useSubmissionHandlers.ts:7`, `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/usePromptQueueHandlers.ts:8`).

Complete-task path handling mixes migration compatibility, path probing, and generation construction with mutable cross-stage params (`supabase/functions/complete_task/params.ts:42`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/params.ts:172`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation-child.ts:41`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation-child.ts:77`).

`src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx:1-71`, `src/shared/components/ui/label.tsx:1-22`, and `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx:1-50` use a no-semicolon/double-quote style island, while nearby app/shared files such as `src/app/hooks/useSettingsModal.ts:1-63` use semicolons and single quotes.
`src/app/routes.tsx:22` uses double quotes for one import while the rest of the file is single-quote style, indicating localized convention drift inside the same module.

`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:57-64` logs missing-shot failures with `console.error` and returns, while the same file uses `handleError(...)` in other failure paths (`:50`, `:70`, `:86`), creating mixed error channels for sibling paths.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:213-216` converts segment query errors into `null`, and `checkOrchestratorCompletion` treats `!allSegments` as a normal early return (`:115-117`), making query failure behavior indistinguishable from valid 'no progress' states.
`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:238-240` maps DB errors to `'not_found'` in `checkFinalStitchStatus`, collapsing operational failures into a domain-state sentinel.
`authenticateRequest` only accepts JWT user auth when `allowJwtUserAuth: true` is passed.

`supabase/functions/generate-upload-url/index.ts` and `supabase/functions/task-counts/index.ts` document `Authorization: Bearer <JWT or PAT>` but call `authenticateRequest(req, supabaseAdmin, ...)` without JWT opt-in.
Other sibling endpoints do opt-in (`supabase/functions/tasks-list/index.ts`, `supabase/functions/create-task/index.ts`), creating inconsistent access behavior.

findings.summary: Core error helper directly depends on UI toast, creating cross-layer coupling.
evidence: `handleError` imports `toast` and conditionally renders destructive toasts inside the core handler.
Feature hooks call `handleError` for operational failures, so their error path implicitly includes presentation behavior from shared UI.
findings.suggestion: Split error handling into a UI-agnostic core (`normalize/log/return`) and an optional UI adapter (`handleErrorWithToast`), then migrate non-UI hooks to the core API and call toast behavior only at explicit UI boundaries.

findings.summary: Deprecated `types/shots` shim is still on active import paths.
evidence: `src/types/shots.ts` is marked deprecated and only re-exports `@/types/generationAndShots`.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers` still imports `Shot` from `@/types/shots`, keeping the legacy boundary alive.
findings.suggestion: Replace remaining imports of `@/types/shots` with `@/types/generationAndShots`, then remove the shim once callers are migrated.

findings.summary: Shared Button primitive mixes design-system and feature-specific style policy.
evidence: `button.tsx` includes broad variant sets (`retro*`, `theme*`, `success`) with page/tool-specific styling comments.
Feature components consume the same global primitive, so feature visual policy changes can force edits in the central shared module.
findings.suggestion: Keep `Button` to baseline variants only, and move feature- or page-specific presets into thin local wrappers (e.g., `HomeButton`, `MediaActionButton`) near their owning domains.

findings.summary: Mobile/touch interaction policy is implemented in multiple unrelated modules.
evidence: `tooltip.tsx` defines touch-specific open/close behavior with pointer listeners.
`useMobileInteractions.ts` separately defines tap/double-tap and outside-close behavior for gallery interactions.
`CreativePartnerPane.tsx` adds custom mobile tooltip toggling/disable rules again at feature level.
findings.suggestion: Define a shared touch-interaction contract (state machine or small hook set) and have tooltip/gallery/home components compose that contract instead of each implementing bespoke tap/open/close semantics.

findings.summary: Image edit state uses a massive pass-through param bag abstraction with low leverage.
evidence: `buildImageEditValueParams` in `useInlineEditState` forwards a very large set of fields from multiple sub-hooks into one object.

`UseImageEditValueParams` in `useImageEditValue` mirrors those fields and mostly reconstructs an object via `buildModeState`/`buildCanvasAndAnnotationState`/`buildRepositionAndPanelState`/`buildFormAndGenerationState` with minimal semantic transformation.
findings.suggestion: Split `ImageEditState` assembly into cohesive slices (mode, canvas, generation) owned by their source hooks, then compose those slices directly in one coordinator hook with a narrower input contract. Remove one-to-one forwarding fields where callers can consume source slices directly.

findings.summary: Legacy/new orchestrator parameter translation is duplicated across frontend payload and backend completion seams.
evidence: `payloadBuilder.ts` translates multiple legacy structure formats into unified `structure_guidance` and then strips legacy fields afterward.
`individualTravelSegment.ts` re-implements compatibility parsing/fallback behavior for overlapping field shapes from original/orchestrator payloads.
`orchestrator.ts` sibling/final-step lookup uses OR chains across multiple param paths for run/task IDs, implying runtime dependency on mixed historical field names.
findings.suggestion: Introduce one canonical orchestration contract version and a single normalization layer at task-ingress (task creation). Persist only canonical keys in `tasks.params`, migrate old rows opportunistically, and simplify completion queries to one key path per concept.

findings.summary: Button-group prop construction uses repetitive copy layers and unstable memo boundaries.
evidence: `useButtonGroupProps.ts` repeats the same shared fields in four builder functions, with only small per-group differences.
`useSharedLightboxState.ts` passes inline nested objects into `useButtonGroupProps`; combined with `useMemo(..., [params])`, this causes recompute on each render and reduces value of the abstraction.
`useSharedLightboxState.ts` wraps `handleUpscale` in `async () => await Promise.resolve(handleUpscale())`, which is an extra pass-through layer.
findings.suggestion: Collapse builders into a single shared base object plus small per-group overrides, memoize on primitive dependencies instead of the entire `params` object, and remove the `Promise.resolve` wrapper unless `handleUpscale` can be sync in practice.

findings.summary: Request builder exposes two overlapping input schemas with implicit precedence.
evidence: `buildTravelRequestBody` defines nested param groups and a second flat legacy parameter set in one public function contract, then resolves by fallback merging (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:463`).

The module also exports compatibility aliases and test-oriented alternate names, increasing surface ambiguity (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:596`).

A similar flattening pattern exists in form prop resolvers that spread multiple groups into one object (`resolveJoinClipsSettingsFormProps`), reinforcing ambiguous precedence at API boundaries (`src/shared/components/JoinClipsSettingsForm/types.ts:106`).
findings.suggestion: Split into a strict v2 request input type used by production code and a small adapter for legacy/test callers. Keep one canonical shape in `buildTravelRequestBody` and move legacy flattening to a separately named compatibility function.

findings.summary: Invalidation APIs mix fire-and-forget and awaitable behavior for similar operations.
evidence: `useInvalidateGenerations` and `invalidateGenerationsSync` can defer work via timers but return `void`, so callers cannot await completion (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:62`, `src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:84`).
`invalidateVariantChange` in the same module is `async` and partially awaits refetches (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:105`).

Adjacent write-sequencing utilities use explicit promise-based completion (`runSerializedTimelineWrite`), creating inconsistent expectations for call sites coordinating writes and invalidations (`src/shared/lib/timelineWriteQueue.ts:108`).
findings.suggestion: Standardize invalidation APIs to return `Promise<void>` whenever delayed or multi-step work can occur. Provide explicit `fireAndForget` wrappers only where intentional, and document completion semantics in function names.

findings.summary: Shot-generation translation logic is duplicated across service seams.
evidence: `generateVideoService` defines local `ShotGenRow`, custom selection query, filtering, and payload assembly (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:151`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:246`).

`generationTransformers` separately defines `RawShotGeneration` and conversion rules for timeline consumption (`src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:163`, `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:379`).
Both modules encode overlapping DB-to-domain translation concerns independently, increasing seam friction when schema fields change.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared shot-generation mapper package (query projection + normalizers + typed DTO) and have both generation submission and timeline/gallery transforms consume that single boundary layer.

findings.summary: Nested legacy schema fallback logic is scattered instead of centralized.
evidence: `extractOrchestratorRef` hardcodes multiple nested param paths for current/legacy formats (`supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts:31`).

`extractPrompt` and `extractPairOverrides` use separate nested fallback chains for related metadata evolution paths (`src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:193`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:40`).
These translators are implemented independently across modules, so migration updates require synchronized manual edits across boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Create shared schema-access helpers for legacy/current task metadata paths and reuse them across frontend transformers and edge-function billing utilities to keep migration logic in one seam.

findings.summary: Legacy travel/image payload fields remain first-class in shared types, extending migration tail risk.
evidence: `legacyStructureVideo.ts` defines multiple deprecated fields (`structure_video_*`, `use_uni3c`, `motion_strength`) with an explicit sunset contract.
`ImageGenerationForm/types.ts` keeps deprecated project-level fields (`selectedReferenceId`, `styleReference*`, etc.) for backward migration compatibility.
`types/shots.ts` remains as a deprecated re-export shim instead of fully removing the old type entrypoint.
findings.suggestion: Set an enforcement path: add lint/codemod checks to block new legacy-field usage, migrate all internal callers to modern structures (`structure_guidance`, `generationAndShots`), and remove shim exports once importer count reaches zero.

findings.summary: Aspect-ratio fallback logic is duplicated across two utilities with partially overlapping responsibilities.
evidence: Both files independently compute `{ aspectRatio: string }` with similar fallback to project ratio and default square.
One path parses only project ratio, while the other also parses metadata/orchestrator resolution, creating drift risk for future behavior changes.
findings.suggestion: Extract one canonical aspect-ratio resolver in `src/shared/lib/imageAspectRatio.ts` and make ShotImageManager call it with any source-specific inputs.

findings.summary: Several small helpers are wrapped in heavy explanatory comments that restate behavior and obscure the operational core.
evidence: `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` includes lengthy explanatory prose and example usage for a small, self-descriptive hook.
`image-utils.ts` includes comment blocks narrating obvious loop and field operations.
`storagePaths.ts` has long scaffolding comments for straightforward string template helpers.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to non-obvious intent/constraints only; keep concise docblocks where API contracts are subtle and remove code-restating narration.

findings.summary: Peripheral test/build config files are scattered at root instead of a single config package.
evidence: All five assigned root config files are flagged as peripheral in structure data, and four have fan_in 0-1.
`vitest.config.ts` depends on `./vitest.edge.config` only to derive include/exclude boundaries, indicating shared concern but split ownership.
Root-level accumulation makes build/test topology discovery depend on filename guessing rather than directory boundary.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create `config/testing/`, `config/build/`, and `config/styles/`; (2) move `vitest.edge.config.ts` and `vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts` to `config/testing/`, then move `vitest.config.ts` to `config/testing/web.vitest.config.ts`; (3) move `vite.config.ts` to `config/build/vite.config.ts` and `tailwind.config.ts` to `config/styles/tailwind.config.ts`; (4) add tiny root shim files only when tool discovery requires root presence; (5) update imports/scripts (`rg "vitest\.edge\.config|vitest\.config|vite\.config|tailwind\.config" -n .` then update package scripts); (6) validate with `pnpm vitest -c config/testing/web.vitest.config.ts`, `pnpm vitest -c config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts`, and `pnpm vite build --config config/build/vite.config.ts`.

findings.summary: A 98-file flat hooks directory mixes unrelated domains and weakens ownership boundaries.
evidence: directory_profiles reports `src/shared/hooks/` with file_count 98, exceeding flat-directory split threshold.
Assigned hooks in the same folder implement distinct responsibilities (AI prompt orchestration, gallery page preloading, API-key persistence) with no common domain boundary in path.

Coupling matrix shows heavy outward edges from shared hooks to multiple infra areas (e.g., `src/shared/hooks/ → src/shared/lib/` 63 and `→ src/integrations/supabase/` 50), reinforcing that the folder is a broad catch-all.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create target folders `src/shared/hooks/ai/`, `src/shared/hooks/gallery/`, and `src/shared/hooks/account/`; (2) move files in this order: `useAIInteractionService.ts` + test, then `useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` + test, then `useApiKeys.ts` + test; (3) convert old paths to temporary re-export shims for one cycle to reduce churn; (4) run import updates via `rg "@/shared/hooks/(useAIInteractionService|useAdjacentPagePreloader|useApiKeys)" -n src` and replace with new paths; (5) validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAIInteractionService.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAdjacentPagePreloader.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useApiKeys.test.ts` and full typecheck.

findings.summary: Test ownership model is split between centralized hook tests and colocated component tests.
evidence: Hooks tests are centralized under `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/`, while component tests are colocated with production files in `src/shared/components/`.
This mixed policy creates two discovery rules for sibling shared packages and weakens predictability of where ownership and verification live.
The inconsistency appears in assigned files directly rather than being isolated to one legacy area.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) choose one policy per package layer (recommended: colocate tests beside units for both hooks and components); (2) move `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAIInteractionService.test.ts` to `src/shared/hooks/ai/useAIInteractionService.test.ts`, `useAdjacentPagePreloader.test.ts` to `src/shared/hooks/gallery/useAdjacentPagePreloader.test.ts`, and `useApiKeys.test.ts` to `src/shared/hooks/account/useApiKeys.test.ts` after hook folder split; (3) keep `src/shared/components/*.test.tsx` colocated; (4) update glob patterns in vitest configs to avoid brittle folder assumptions; (5) validate with `pnpm vitest --run` and `rg "__tests__/use(AIInteractionService|AdjacentPagePreloader|ApiKeys)" -n src` to confirm no stale paths.

findings.summary: Two high-fan-in hubs mix boundary roles and side effects, raising codebase blast radius.
evidence: `handleError` in shared lib normalizes/logs errors and also calls UI toast directly.
`supabase` client module performs instrumentation/runtime setup during import (`export const supabase = getOrCreateSupabaseClient()`).
Both files are hub modules in holistic context (225 and 146 importers).
findings.suggestion: Split each hub into explicit boundary contracts: (1) move pure error normalization/logging into `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/core.ts` and create a UI adapter (`src/shared/components/errorHandling/toastError.ts`) used only by presentation layers; (2) expose `getSupabaseClient()` as lazy retrieval and move runtime/instrumentation bootstrap into app startup (`src/app/bootstrap.tsx`) so imports are side-effect-free; then run `npm run typecheck` and `npm run test` to validate no import-cycle or init-order regressions.

findings.summary: Deprecated type alias policy is declared but not operationally enforced.
evidence: `src/types/shots.ts` includes explicit deprecation/sunset metadata with removal date `2026-05-31`.
Holistic context marks this file as a 199-importer hub, so the deprecated path is still one of the dominant contracts.
`README.md` emphasizes architecture/document trust, but this contract drift weakens that trust.
findings.suggestion: Adopt an enforced deprecation lifecycle: add a CI gate that fails new imports of `@/types/shots`, publish migration ownership in docs, and execute a phased codemod from `@/types/shots` to `@/types/generationAndShots` before the dated removal window.

findings.summary: A high-fan-in generic utils barrel in an overloaded directory obscures package ownership.
evidence: `src/shared/lib/` has 70 files, indicating flat mixed-concern organization.
`src/shared/lib/utils.ts` is a 134-importer hub but only re-exports from two different concern areas (`uiClassNames` and `stringFormatting`).
`button.tsx` imports from `@/shared/lib/utils`, reinforcing broad generic utility coupling instead of concern-specific modules.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: create `src/shared/lib/ui/cn.ts` and `src/shared/lib/text/stringFormatting.ts` (or align with existing `stringFormatting.ts` path) and keep temporary compatibility exports. Stage 2 move order: (a) move/alias `cn` first, (b) update UI primitives like `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx`, (c) migrate string helper consumers, (d) delete `src/shared/lib/utils.ts` last. Stage 3 import updates and validation commands: `rg -l "@/shared/lib/utils" src | xargs -I{} sed -i '' -e "s#@/shared/lib/utils#@/shared/lib/ui/cn#g" -e "s#@/shared/lib/utils#@/shared/lib/text/stringFormatting#g" {}` (or repo-preferred codemod), then run `npm run typecheck` and `npm run test`.

findings.summary: Timeline orchestrator hook owns too many unrelated responsibilities in one unit.
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` defines broad props/return contracts and then directly coordinates drag, zoom, global events, pending frame bookkeeping, drop interception, duplicate placement logic, and structure-video selection (`.../useTimelineOrchestrator.ts:21`, `:54`, `:141`, `:414`, `:504`).

The hook imports multiple specialized hooks but still contains substantial domain logic and state transitions itself, so decomposition did not isolate responsibilities (`.../useTimelineOrchestrator.ts:6-13`, `:346`, `:473`).
findings.suggestion: Split into explicit coordinators: `useTimelineInteractionState` (drag/zoom/selection), `useTimelinePlacementOps` (duplicate/drop/frame placement rules), and `useStructureVideoSelection`. Keep `useTimelineOrchestrator` as a thin composition layer that wires outputs together without business logic.

findings.summary: Shared lightbox state collapses many domains behind one oversized facade interface.
evidence: `UseSharedLightboxStateProps` extends many grouped interfaces and feeds a single hook that assembles variants, navigation, star, references/join, lineage, shots, source generation, layout, and button props (`.../useSharedLightboxState.ts:128`, `:602`).

`MediaLightbox` and `ImageLightbox` must pass/reshape large grouped bundles to satisfy this facade, indicating integration friction from interface breadth (`.../MediaLightbox.tsx:11`, `:79`, `:148`; `.../ImageLightbox.tsx` shared-state call path).
findings.suggestion: Replace one mega hook with independent state modules consumed directly by image/video lightboxes (e.g., `useLightboxVariants`, `useLightboxShotActions`, `useLightboxLayout`, `useLightboxButtonActions`), then compose only required modules per mode.

findings.summary: Image edit orchestrator mixes mode orchestration and context construction into a god hook.
evidence: A single hook accepts a large config surface and drives inpainting, magic-edit, reposition, and img2img flows in one execution path (`.../useImageEditOrchestrator.ts:32`, `:128`, `:176`, `:262`, `:307`, `:350`).

It also builds a very large memoized `ImageEditState` object and includes placeholder values (`isFlippedHorizontally`, `isSaving`) inside the same unit, blending orchestration and state-shaping concerns (`.../useImageEditOrchestrator.ts:392`).
findings.suggestion: Move each mode into independently exported adapters that emit a normalized `ModeController` contract, then build `ImageEditContext` in a dedicated mapper function. Keep orchestrator limited to selecting active mode + composing shared controls.

findings.summary: Image generation submission uses a wide prop bag forwarded through multiple layers.
evidence: `UseFormSubmissionProps` includes many operational fields and callbacks in one interface (`.../types.ts:33` onward), then `useFormSubmission` destructures and re-passes most of them (`.../useFormSubmission.ts:20`, `:93`).

`useSubmissionHandlers` and `usePromptQueueHandlers` repeat nearly the same input bag shape, showing structural duplication and weak ownership boundaries (`.../useSubmissionHandlers.ts:7`, `:24`; `.../usePromptQueueHandlers.ts:8`, `:31`).
findings.suggestion: Define explicit domain payloads (`PromptGenerationContext`, `TaskCreationContext`, `QueueExecutionContext`) and pass only the subset each handler needs. Add one adapter at the boundary from UI state to these smaller contexts.

findings.summary: Tasks pane component mixes pane UI, filtering UX, and data mutation orchestration.
evidence: `TasksPane` manages pane animation behavior, filter/pagination state, query orchestration, optimistic cancellation mutation, and lightbox integration in one component (`.../TasksPane.tsx:39`, `:125`, `:158`, `:239`, `:322`).

`useTasks` independently includes query builders, custom processing sort policy, and freshness reporting side effects, creating split-but-overlapping orchestration responsibilities (`.../useTasks.ts:93`, `:117`, `:209`).
findings.suggestion: Extract a `useTasksPaneController` for task-domain orchestration (filters, pagination, cancellation, lightbox intents) and keep `TasksPane` mostly presentational. Keep data policy (sorting/count rules) in `useTasks` only, then expose a cleaner view model.

findings.summary: Complete-task flow spreads param-shape compatibility logic across modules and stages.
evidence: `params.ts` repeatedly probes many alternate nested paths for equivalent fields and performs deep clone mutation (`.../params.ts:42`, `:106`, `:125`, `:172`), indicating unresolved canonical param schema.

`generation-child.ts` mutates `taskData.params` mid-flow and re-extracts nested fields (including orchestrator arrays), coupling migration normalization with generation creation logic (`.../generation-child.ts:41`, `:77`, `:93`, `:498`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single normalization step in `index.ts` that converts incoming task params to one canonical typed shape. Pass that normalized structure through downstream handlers as immutable data and remove per-handler path probing.

findings.summary: Sibling failure paths mix `console.error`+return and centralized `handleError` handling.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` uses `handleError` in catch blocks (`:50`, `:70`) but uses raw `console.error` and silent return for missing shot/fullShot (`:57-64`).
The shared contract in `handleError.ts` normalizes/logs/toasts errors, but these branches bypass it entirely.
findings.suggestion: Route missing-shot branches through `handleError(new Error(...), { context: 'MediaGallery', showToast: false })` (or a shared non-throwing reporter) so all gallery handler failures follow one logging/diagnostic contract.

findings.summary: Orchestrator DB query failures are downgraded to normal control-flow states.
evidence: `findSiblingSegments` logs query errors then returns `null` (`orchestrator.ts:213-216`), and caller exits early on `!allSegments` (`:115-117`) without marking failure/retry.
`checkFinalStitchStatus` returns `'not_found'` on query error (`:238-240`), merging operational failure with legitimate absence.
In contrast, `calculate-task-cost` returns explicit error responses on DB/auth failures (`calculate-task-cost/index.ts:229-233`, `:320-324`, `:461-469`).
findings.suggestion: Use a discriminated return (`{ kind: 'ok'|'not_found'|'error', ... }`) or throw on DB errors in orchestrator helpers; propagate to a caller path that records failure/retry intent instead of early-returning as if no data existed.

findings.summary: Formatting conventions drift across sibling shared/app modules.
evidence: `button.tsx`, `tooltip.tsx`, `label.tsx`, and `useMobile.tsx` largely follow double-quote/no-semicolon style.
`useSettingsModal.ts` is single-quote/semicolon style, and `routes.tsx` contains a mixed-style outlier import at `:22` within an otherwise single-quote file.
findings.suggestion: Enforce one formatter/lint contract repo-wide (or explicitly per-directory) and normalize this batch’s outliers so style does not vary by file provenance.

findings.summary: JWT support is documented for multiple edge endpoints but disabled in actual auth calls.
evidence: `authenticateRequest` requires `allowJwtUserAuth: true` to accept JWT user tokens.
`generate-upload-url` and `task-counts` headers/docs state `Bearer <JWT or PAT>` but call `authenticateRequest(req, supabaseAdmin, ...)` without options.
`tasks-list` and `create-task` pass `{ allowJwtUserAuth: true }`, showing sibling divergence.
findings.suggestion: Normalize auth policy per endpoint class and enforce it in one helper: add explicit auth mode constants (service-only, pat-or-jwt-user, pat-only), wire each endpoint through that helper, and add integration tests for JWT and PAT paths on all user-facing endpoints.

findings.summary: Sibling edge APIs expose inconsistent error/response envelopes and CORS handling.
evidence: `get-task-status` returns plain text errors (`new Response("...")`) while `tasks-list` uses JSON envelopes via `jsonResponse`.
`create-task` uses custom `createCorsResponse` string bodies instead of shared response utility.
This creates client-side branching by endpoint for equivalent failure categories.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a single shared edge response contract (`{ error, code, details? }` for failures and JSON success envelopes) and a shared CORS+JSON helper module; migrate high-traffic endpoints first (`create-task`, `get-task-status`, `generate-upload-url`) and validate with endpoint contract tests.

findings.summary: Legacy/new model coexistence persists across types, metadata migration logic, and tool facades.
evidence: `src/types/shots.ts` is explicitly deprecated with remove-by date but remains heavily imported per packet context.
`segmentSettingsMigration.ts` supports multiple generations of metadata fields (`segmentOverrides`, `pair_*`, `user_overrides`) in active path.
Compatibility facades still preserve old imports instead of forcing canonical module usage.
findings.suggestion: Run a migration completion plan: 1) freeze new legacy usages with lint/import rules, 2) codemod all `@/types/shots` imports to `@/types/generationAndShots`, 3) move legacy readers into an isolated `legacy/` boundary, 4) remove facade exports once callers hit zero, and 5) enforce with CI checks on deprecated symbol usage.

findings.summary: Multiple compatibility facades add import indirection with little local behavior.
evidence: `applySettingsService.ts` is largely a re-export shell over `services/applySettings/*` modules.
`tools/image-generation/.../ImageGenerationForm/index.tsx` exists mainly as backward-compat re-export to shared implementation.
This pattern repeats in tool-local wrappers and increases mental overhead for locating authoritative logic.
findings.suggestion: Define canonical entrypoints per domain (for example `shared/*` or `tools/*`), deprecate wrapper modules with automated import rewrites, then remove wrappers in a timed batch after import counts reach zero.

findings.summary: Large mixed-concern shared directories and distributed compatibility shims hurt navigability and ownership clarity.
evidence: From `holistic_context.structure`: `src/shared/components/` has 80 files and 10 import-source directories; `src/shared/hooks/` has 98 production files and 10-direction coupling.
From coupling matrix: heavy cross-edges (`shared/components -> shared/components/ui` 111, `shared/components -> shared/hooks` 58, `shared/hooks -> shared/lib` 63) indicate broad entanglement.
Compatibility concerns are scattered across `src/types`, `src/tools`, and `src/shared/components` instead of a bounded migration package.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: create `src/domains/image-generation/`, `src/domains/video-travel/`, and `src/legacy/compat/` for temporary adapters; Stage 2 move order: move leaf/shared feature modules first (ImageGenerationForm internals, ShotEditor applySettings services), then move compatibility re-exports/types (`src/types/shots.ts`, tool wrappers) into `src/legacy/compat/`; Stage 3 import update: run codemods and alias updates, then `rg -n "@/types/shots|applySettingsService|tools/.*/components/ImageGenerationForm" src`; Stage 4 validation commands: `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm -s vitest run`, and a smoke run of edge-function tests to verify API paths.
